title: Mechanism Exemption Registry
description: Registry of internal mechanisms that require automatic invocation and are therefore exempt from G-13/GG-31 policy requirements. Each entry documents why the mechanism cannot be implemented as a primitive and verifies it preserves determinism.

exemptions.name: State Version Counter
exemptions.description: A u64 version counter that increments automatically on every state modification (set, set_all, remove, set_path). Used for O(1) state change detection in execute_until_stable.
exemptions.why_not_primitive: Requires automatic invocation on every state modification. Implementing as a primitive would require every JSON rule that modifies state to explicitly call a bump_version primitive, which is error-prone and places burden on rule authors.
exemptions.determinism_guarantee: Same input always produces same output. Version counter is purely an internal tracking mechanism and does not affect the state data itself.
exemptions.audit_impact: None. The version counter is not recorded in the audit chain; it is only used for change detection between iterations.
exemptions.performance_benefit: O(1) state change detection instead of O(n) full state comparison.

exemptions.name: Persistent HashMap Structural Sharing
exemptions.description: Use of im::HashMap for State's internal data storage, providing O(1) clone operations via structural sharing.
exemptions.why_not_primitive: This is a data structure property that works automatically. There is no 'callable' operation - the structural sharing happens transparently when clone() is called.
exemptions.determinism_guarantee: Same input always produces same output. im::HashMap maintains the same iteration order as std::HashMap for the same insertion sequence.
exemptions.audit_impact: None. Structural sharing is transparent to the audit chain.
exemptions.performance_benefit: O(1) clone operations instead of O(n) deep copies, critical for efficient state management during rule execution.

exemptions.name: Deterministic Iteration Order
exemptions.description: State::keys() and State::values() return sorted keys to ensure deterministic iteration order (ER-601 compliance).
exemptions.why_not_primitive: Deterministic iteration is a fundamental requirement of the State API. Implementing this as a primitive would require every rule that iterates over state to explicitly call a sort_keys primitive, violating ER-601.
exemptions.determinism_guarantee: Keys are always returned in sorted order, ensuring deterministic iteration regardless of insertion order.
exemptions.audit_impact: None. Sorted iteration is transparent to the audit chain.
exemptions.performance_benefit: Enables deterministic rule execution, a core requirement of the TCB.

exemptions.name: Audit Chain HMAC Linking
exemptions.description: Each audit record automatically includes a hash of the previous record, forming an HMAC-linked chain.
exemptions.why_not_primitive: HMAC linking must happen automatically on every audit record creation. Implementing as a primitive would require every rule that generates audit records to explicitly call a link_audit primitive.
exemptions.determinism_guarantee: Same sequence of operations produces the same hash chain.
exemptions.audit_impact: Directly impacts the audit chain format. The HMAC link is part of every audit record.
exemptions.performance_benefit: Ensures tamper-proof audit records - modifying any record breaks the hash chain.

exemptions.name: Registry Freeze Protection
exemptions.description: Automatic check on every primitive registry modification attempt to ensure the registry is not frozen.
exemptions.why_not_primitive: Freeze protection must be enforced at the engine level, not at the rule level. Rules should not be able to bypass freeze checks.
exemptions.determinism_guarantee: Same input produces same output. Freeze checks only prevent modifications; they don't affect execution.
exemptions.audit_impact: None. Freeze checks are internal validation.
exemptions.performance_benefit: Prevents runtime modification of the instruction registry, ensuring consistent execution.

exemptions.name: Instruction Queue FIFO Ordering
exemptions.description: Instruction queue maintains strict FIFO ordering for deterministic execution.
exemptions.why_not_primitive: Queue ordering is a fundamental requirement of the execution model. There is no 'callable' operation - the ordering is enforced automatically.
exemptions.determinism_guarantee: Instructions are always processed in FIFO order, ensuring deterministic execution.
exemptions.audit_impact: None. Queue ordering is transparent to the audit chain.
exemptions.performance_benefit: Enables deterministic execution flow, a core requirement of the TCB.

exemptions.name: Dispatch Table Auto-Projection
exemptions.description: Automatically builds the dispatch cases table from the InstructionRegistry at startup.
exemptions.why_not_primitive: The dispatch table must be built once at startup, not on every rule invocation. It is a configuration-time operation, not a runtime operation.
exemptions.determinism_guarantee: Same registry produces the same dispatch table.
exemptions.audit_impact: The dispatch table hash is recorded in audit records for version tracking.
exemptions.performance_benefit: Eliminates manual maintenance of the dispatch cases table in JSON.

exemptions.name: While Loop Safety Valve
exemptions.description: Automatic max_steps limit to prevent infinite loops.
exemptions.why_not_primitive: The safety valve must be enforced at the while_loop level, not at the rule level. It is a mechanism-level protection, not a policy decision.
exemptions.determinism_guarantee: Same input produces same output, with early termination if max_steps is reached.
exemptions.audit_impact: None. The safety valve is transparent to the audit chain.
exemptions.performance_benefit: Prevents infinite loops due to malformed rules.

review_process.description: All mechanism exemptions must be reviewed and approved by the architecture review team before being added to this registry.

steps.title: Submit Proposal
steps.description: Create a draft entry with all required fields and a clear justification for why the mechanism cannot be implemented as a primitive.

steps.title: Determinism Review
steps.description: Verify the mechanism preserves determinism (same input → same output).

steps.title: Policy Review
steps.description: Verify the mechanism does not encode any business policy or configurable thresholds.

steps.title: Audit Review
steps.description: Verify the mechanism does not affect audit chain transparency.

steps.title: Approval
steps.description: Approved by architecture review team and added to the registry.
